audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Joshua / Yehoshua east-Jordan tribes' release and altar-dispute de-escalation narrative, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Transjordan tribes' fulfilled obligation, blessing and return, large altar at Geliloth, western war mobilization, Phinehas delegation, Peor/Achan memories, land/altar anxiety, witness-altar explanation, successful de-escalation, and naming of the altar.

verses.restored: Then Joshua called Reubenites, Gadites, and half tribe of Manasseh.
He said to them, You have kept all that Moses servant of YHWH commanded you, and you have listened to my voice in all I commanded you.
You have not abandoned your brothers these many days to this day, and you have kept charge of command of YHWH your Divine.
Now YHWH your Divine has given rest to your brothers as he spoke to them; now turn and go for yourselves to your tents, to land of your possession that Moses servant of YHWH gave you across Jordan.

Only be very careful to do command and Torah that Moses servant of YHWH commanded you: to love YHWH your Divine, walk in all his ways, keep his commands, cling to him, and serve him with all your heart and all your soul/life.
Joshua blessed them and sent them away, and they went to their tents.
To half tribe of Manasseh Moses had given in Bashan, and to its other half Joshua gave with their brothers across Jordan westward. When Joshua sent them to their tents, he blessed them,
and spoke to them, saying, Return to your tents with much wealth, very many livestock, silver, gold, bronze, iron, and very many garments; divide spoil of your enemies with your brothers.

Sons of Reuben, sons of Gad, and half tribe of Manasseh returned and went from children of Israel at Shiloh in land of Canaan to land of Gilead, land of their possession, in which they had been possessed according to mouth of YHWH by hand of Moses.
They came to districts of Jordan that are in land of Canaan, and sons of Reuben, sons of Gad, and half tribe of Manasseh built there an altar by Jordan, an altar great in appearance.
Children of Israel heard, saying, Look, sons of Reuben, sons of Gad, and half tribe of Manasseh built altar opposite land of Canaan, in districts of Jordan, across from children of Israel.
When children of Israel heard, all congregation of children of Israel assembled at Shiloh to go up against them for war.
Children of Israel sent to sons of Reuben, sons of Gad, and half tribe of Manasseh, to land of Gilead, Phinehas son of Eleazar the priest,
and ten leaders with him, one leader from fathers' house for each tribe of Israel; each one was head of house of their fathers among thousands of Israel.
They came to sons of Reuben, sons of Gad, and half tribe of Manasseh in land of Gilead and spoke with them, saying,

Thus says all congregation of YHWH: What is this unfaithfulness that you have acted against Divine of Israel, turning today from after YHWH, by building for yourselves an altar, to rebel today against YHWH?
Is iniquity of Peor too little for us, from which we have not cleansed ourselves to this day, though plague came upon congregation of YHWH,
that you turn today from after YHWH? It shall be, if you rebel today against YHWH, tomorrow he will be angry with all congregation of Israel.

But if land of your possession is unclean, cross over to land of possession of YHWH, where tabernacle of YHWH dwells, and take possession in our midst; only do not rebel against YHWH and do not rebel against us by building for yourselves altar besides altar of YHWH our Divine.
Did not Achan son of Zerah act unfaithfully concerning devoted thing, and wrath came upon all congregation of Israel? He did not perish alone in his iniquity.
Then sons of Reuben, sons of Gad, and half tribe of Manasseh answered and spoke to heads of thousands of Israel:
Divine of divines, YHWH, Divine of divines, YHWH, he knows, and Israel shall know: if in rebellion or if in unfaithfulness against YHWH, do not save us this day!
If we built for ourselves an altar to turn from after YHWH, or to offer upon it burnt offering or grain offering, or to make upon it sacrifices of well-being, YHWH himself shall seek it out.
But truly from concern, from a thing, we did this, saying, Tomorrow your children may say to our children, What is there to you and to YHWH Divine of Israel?
For YHWH has placed Jordan as border between us and you, sons of Reuben and sons of Gad; you have no portion in YHWH. So your children might make our children cease from fearing/revering YHWH.
So we said, Let us now build for ourselves an altar—not for burnt offering and not for sacrifice,

but to be witness between us and you and between our generations after us, that we may perform service of YHWH before him with our burnt offerings, sacrifices, and well-being offerings, and your children shall not say tomorrow to our children, You have no portion in YHWH.

We said, It shall be, if they say thus to us and to our generations tomorrow, then we shall say, See pattern/copy of altar of YHWH that our fathers made, not for burnt offering and not for sacrifice, but witness between us and you.

Far be it from us to rebel against YHWH and turn today from after YHWH by building altar for burnt offering, grain offering, or sacrifice, besides altar of YHWH our Divine that is before his tabernacle.
When Phinehas the priest and leaders of congregation, heads of thousands of Israel who were with him, heard words sons of Reuben, sons of Gad, and sons of Manasseh spoke, it was good in their eyes.

Phinehas son of Eleazar the priest said to sons of Reuben, sons of Gad, and sons of Manasseh, Today we know YHWH is in our midst, because you have not acted this unfaithfulness against YHWH; now you have rescued children of Israel from hand of YHWH.
Phinehas son of Eleazar the priest and leaders returned from sons of Reuben and sons of Gad, from land of Gilead to land of Canaan, to children of Israel, and brought back word to them.
The word was good in eyes of children of Israel, and children of Israel blessed Divine and did not speak of going up against them for war, to destroy land where sons of Reuben and sons of Gad dwelt.
Sons of Reuben and sons of Gad called altar Witness, for it is witness between us that YHWH is Divine.

translation_notes: The east-Jordan tribes fulfill their military obligation and are released with blessing, wealth, and a renewed charge to love, walk, keep, cling, and serve.
The altar crisis shows how quickly religious anxiety and inherited trauma can mobilize a community toward civil war.
The western tribes choose delegation before attack, sending Phinehas and ten leaders to investigate. This procedural pause prevents bloodshed.
The accusations invoke Peor and Achan, memories of corporate catastrophe that could have escalated panic and collective punishment.
The east-Jordan explanation is about future belonging: they fear the Jordan border will let later generations say, 'You have no portion in YHWH.'
The altar is not a rival sacrificial site but a witness/copy that protects covenant inclusion across geography.
The chapter is one of Joshua's strongest de-escalation narratives: accusation is heard, explanation is accepted, war is stopped, and the altar becomes testimony rather than rebellion.
This successful outcome must not erase the threat of destruction that nearly arose from religious misunderstanding.
safety_note: Do not use Joshua 22 to justify religious vigilantism, preemptive war, civil conflict, or collective punishment based on suspected impurity. Center investigation before action, listening to feared minorities, honoring geographic diversity within shared covenant, and accepting explanations that prevent violence.
